Skill level.name: 1 - Novice
Skill level.description: Familiar with programming and open-source/collaborative development concepts but lacks practical experience

Skill level.name: 2 - Beginner
Skill level.description: Writes basic code, applies foundational practices, and may need guidance for open-source/collaborative workflows

Skill level.name: 3 - Intermediate
Skill level.description: Develops functional software, actively implements guidelines, and debugs independently.

Skill level.name: 4 - Advanced
Skill level.description: Fully integrates best practices into workflows, mentors others, and contributes to collaborative projects.

Skill level.name: 5 - Expert
Skill level.description: Drives best practices across teams, sets standards for open-source/collaborative development, and contributes to community tools or frameworks.

Software Development Model (SDM).name: Open-Source
Software Development Model (SDM).description:
Address Open-Source Software Development Guidelines.

Open-Source Guidelines focus on licensing, documentation, testing, distribution, and compliance with standards (e.g., Argo formats, FAIR principles).

Software Development Model (SDM).name: Collaborative
Software Development Model (SDM).description:
Address Collaborative Software Development Guidelines.

Collaborative Guidelines emphasize teamwork, version control, communication, and project management (e.g., Git workflows, meetings, issue tracking).

Scope.name: Argo specific
Scope.description: This item is specific to software for the Argo RI

Scope.name: General guideline
Scope.description: This item would apply to any software

Project Aspects.name: Location
Project Aspects.description: About where to store and manage the project content

Project Aspects.name: Content
Project Aspects.description: About the repository content and organisation

Project Aspects.name: Management
Project Aspects.description: About how the project and its codebase are managed, possibly collectively

Project Aspects.name: Codebase
Project Aspects.description: About the codebase design and organisation

Project Aspects.name: Documentation and Outreach
Project Aspects.description: About the project and codebase documentation and outreach (communication and dissemination)

Project Aspects.name: Argo compliance
Project Aspects.description: About making use of all Argo tools

FAIR4RS.name: Findable
FAIR4RS.description: Software, and its associated metadata, is easy for both humans and machines to find

FAIR4RS.name: Accessible
FAIR4RS.description: Software, and its metadata, is retrievable via standardised protocols

FAIR4RS.name: Interoperable
FAIR4RS.description: Software interoperates with other software by exchanging data and/or metadata, through interaction via application programming interfaces (APIs), described through standards

FAIR4RS.name: Reusable
FAIR4RS.description: Software is both usable (can be executed) and reusable (can be understood, modified, built upon, or incorporated into other software)

Argo FAIR tools.name: Findable
Argo FAIR tools.description: eg: Input data are uniquely identifiable, i.e. based on Argo DOI and GDAC snapshot DOIs (persistent identifiers), Argo Netcdf and Vocabulary/Conventions (rich metadata), GDAC index files (index of data repositories)

Argo FAIR tools.name: Accessible
Argo FAIR tools.description: eg: Input data are fetched from public http, ftp, s3 GDAC servers, Vocabulary web-API (standardised, open and free protocols)

Argo FAIR tools.name: Interoperable
Argo FAIR tools.description: eg: Output data follow Argo conventions and vocabulary

Argo FAIR tools.name: Reusable
Argo FAIR tools.description: eg: Output data preserve CC BY 4.0 License

Status.name: Documented
Status.description: This item has been sorted, i.e. assigned appropriate labels and documentation

Status.name: Archived
Status.description: This item is no longer in use
